items.title: HCFO-1233zd Market Projected to Reach US$ 71.34 Million by 2035 | Astute Analytica
items.author: Astute Analytica
items.description: The global HCFO-1233zd market is experiencing rapid growth driven by regulatory mandates phasing out high-GWP refrigerants and blowing agents. The market is projected to grow from $38.18 million in 2025 to $71.34 million by 2035 at a 6.4% CAGR. North America leads with 37% market share, while insulation foam blowing agents represent the largest application segment at 51.1%. Major producers are expanding capacity to meet surging demand from construction, data centers, and industrial heat pump applications.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Arkema executed a $60 million investment to launch a 15 kt/year HCFO-1233zd production facility in Kentucky in August 2025, demonstrating aggressive capacity expansion to capitalize on regulatory-driven market growth. The company is well-positioned to serve the booming polyurethane foam sector.

Honeywell's recent spin-off of its Advanced Materials division generated $3.8 billion in sales and $1.1 billion EBITDA, underscoring strong commercial strength in HCFO-1233zd isomers. The company maintains substantial U.S.-based assets generating significant returns from this growing market.

As a major player in the HCFO-1233zd market, Chemours benefits from regulatory mandates driving demand for low-GWP alternatives to legacy HFCs, positioning the company to capture market share in this expanding sector.

Trane has universally adopted HCFO-1233zd for its 5,000+ Series E centrifugal chiller units to meet non-flammable safety codes in hospitals and data centers, aligning with regulatory requirements and benefiting from the growing demand for efficient cooling solutions.

Whirlpool has fully converted its manufacturing lines to use HCFO-1233zd and invested $3 million to meet DOE 2025 energy standards, demonstrating commitment to regulatory compliance and positioning itself to benefit from the market's growth trajectory.
